text: Git branching is a core part of version control that helps teams work together efficiently. When developing a new feature, you create a separate feature branch from the main codebase. This allows you to make changes without affecting the stable version. Once the feature is complete, you submit a pull request to propose merging your changes back into the main branch. A colleague reviews the code, suggests improvements, and then approves the merge. After merging, the branch is usually deleted. This workflow keeps the project organized and reduces conflicts, making collaboration smoother and safer for everyone involved.
